meanings.definition: the particular occupation for which you are trained
assign a specified (usually proper) proper name to
examples: They named their son David
The new school was named after the famous Civil Rights leader

meanings.definition: ascribe a quality to or give a name of a common noun that reflects a quality
examples: He called me a bastard
She called her children lazy and ungrateful

meanings.definition: get or try to get into communication (with someone) by telephone
examples: I tried to call you all night
Take two aspirin and call me in the morning

meanings.definition: utter a sudden loud cry
examples: she cried with pain when the doctor inserted the needle
I yelled to her from the window but she couldn't hear me

meanings.definition: order, request, or command to come
examples: She was called into the director's office
Call the police!

meanings.definition: pay a brief visit
examples: The mayor likes to call on some of the prominent citizens

meanings.definition: call a meeting; invite or command to meet
examples: The Wannsee Conference was called to discuss the `Final Solution'
The new dean calls meetings every week

meanings.definition: read aloud to check for omissions or absentees
examples: Call roll

meanings.definition: send a message or attempt to reach someone by radio, phone, etc.; make a signal to in order to transmit a message
examples: Hawaii is calling!
A transmitter in Samoa was heard calling

meanings.definition: utter a characteristic note or cry
examples: bluejays called to one another

meanings.definition: stop or postpone because of adverse conditions, such as bad weather
examples: call a football game

meanings.definition: greet, as with a prescribed form, title, or name
examples: He always addresses me with `Sir'
Call me Mister
She calls him by first name

meanings.definition: make a stop in a harbour
examples: The ship will call in Honolulu tomorrow

meanings.definition: demand payment of (a loan)
examples: Call a loan

meanings.definition: make a demand, as for a card or a suit or a show of hands
examples: He called his trump

meanings.definition: give the calls (to the dancers) for a square dance
indicate a decision in regard to
examples: call balls and strikes behind the plate

meanings.definition: make a prediction about; tell in advance
examples: Call the outcome of an election

meanings.definition: require the presentation of for redemption before maturation
examples: Call a bond

meanings.definition: challenge (somebody) to make good on a statement; charge with or censure for an offense
examples: He deserves to be called on that

meanings.definition: declare in the capacity of an umpire or referee
examples: call a runner out

meanings.definition: lure by imitating the characteristic call of an animal
examples: Call ducks

meanings.definition: order or request or give a command for
examples: The unions called a general strike for Sunday

meanings.definition: order, summon, or request for a specific duty or activity, work, role
examples: He was already called 4 times for jury duty
They called him to active military duty

meanings.definition: utter in a loud voice or announce
examples: He called my name
The auctioneer called the bids

meanings.definition: challenge the sincerity or truthfulness of
examples: call the speaker on a question of fact

meanings.definition: consider or regard as being
examples: I would not call her beautiful

meanings.definition: rouse somebody from sleep with a call
examples: I was called at 5 A.M. this morning
